story: "Let me protect the dignity of Japan" — 37 votes. A throwaway joke, escalated to a matter of state.
commentary: Escalated a joke into a matter of national honor. Nobody asked him to personally defend Japan's dignity that day, and yet he suited up anyway.

story: "Bolsa?" — 26 votes. Claimed he barely speaks Spanish, then nailed a cashier roleplay opposite Professor Inés Alegre off that one word.
commentary: Claimed he barely speaks Spanish, then nailed a cashier bit opposite an actual professor well enough to win off one word. Somebody was underselling something.

story: "I did the numbers for this. But I don't remember them." — 32 votes. The IESE MBA experience in one sentence.
commentary: "I did the numbers but I don't remember them" is basically the IESE MBA experience printed on a t-shirt. The most self-aware sentence this cohort has produced, and entirely by accident.

story: Framed by Professor Chadwick Miller as Roblox's typical customer during the marketing case: "poor, cheap son of a gun who is under 13." 28 votes.
commentary: A marketing professor, mid-case on Roblox, looked at a grown MBA candidate and decided he was the platonic ideal of a broke twelve-year-old. Akshat didn't even have to say anything — the professor built the entire bit unprompted.

story: "When I first met Ramon he couldn't speak English." — 27 votes, with Ramon sitting right there.
commentary: Told the room Ramon used to struggle with English, with Ramon sitting right there. Loyalty clocked out early that day.

story: "What the f*ck?" — 22 votes. Said out loud in class, professor present.
commentary: Dropped a "what the f*ck" with a professor standing right there, no flinch, no follow-up. Second Tobu for sheer nerve.

story: Updated his LinkedIn to credit Professor Mirel Yavuz with teaching him "list-making" as a skill — then took Tobu to his first marathon.
commentary: Updated his LinkedIn to credit a professor with teaching him "list-making" as a hard skill. Somewhere, a recruiter is very confused.

story: Awarded on the spot for "mouth to mouth communication." No one can explain it anymore.
commentary: Said something about "mouth to mouth communication" that nobody, including him, can reconstruct anymore. Won purely on collective confusion.

story: Spring Break special — 39 votes for the study guides that carried the section through exams. Tobu's parent for the break.
commentary: Won for actually being useful — his study guides got the section through Spring Break. Feels like a clerical error on this list.

story: "If I understood correctly, I didn't get it." — 28 votes for a sentence that cancels itself out mid-thought.
commentary: "If I understood correctly, I didn't get it" cancels itself out mid-sentence and still landed 28 votes. Nobody questioned it, which is the real punchline.

story: "I think Alex S is wrong…" — 25 votes, said directly to Alex Serrano. Reportedly agreed with him an hour later.
commentary: Told Alex Serrano to his face he was wrong, banked 25 votes of backup, then reportedly folded and agreed with him later that day. Strong opinions, short shelf life.

story: "Unsophisticated…simple….people" — 19 votes, delivered in a room full of people paying six figures to debate spreadsheets.
commentary: Called someone else unsophisticated and simple, in a room full of people paying six figures to argue about spreadsheets. The irony detector in that room was clearly broken.

story: "I was on a shitty team" — 20 votes. The Bad Thomas era, part two.
commentary: Blamed the team instead of himself, again. Bad Thomas isn't an accident anymore, it's a brand.

story: Year-end honor: voted Tobu's favorite parent of the year by the whole section — earned the right to bring him home for the summer.
commentary: Ran the weekly polls all year, then got voted favorite parent by the same people she was polling. Convenient how that worked out.
